lead.title: Deportations by India Cause Tension Along Border with Bangladesh
lead.snippet: India is driving out Bangladeshi immigrants it says are in the country illegally. Bangladesh is pushing back, leaving people stranded along the border amid increasing tensions between the countries.

items.title: U.S. and Iran Reignite War Over Strait of Hormuz
items.snippet: The two countries slid back to open war over the waterway, as the U.S. planned to reinstate a blockade on Iranian ports on Tuesday. Iran said it had attacked two tankers and fired at U.S. military sites.

items.title: China Purges Top Official Over Corruption and Sex Charges
items.snippet: Ma Xingrui is the third member of the Politburo to be purged since 2022. He once led the Xinjiang region and was previously head of China’s space program.

items.title: Europe, Flexing Muscle in Ukraine, Is Still on Sidelines in Iran
items.snippet: In a show of collective strength, President Emmanuel Macron of France hosted European allies at the annual Bastille Day parade in Paris. Yet Europe is still a hostage to America’s foreign policy.

items.title: In Bangkok Bar Blaze, Signs of Fatal Lapses Repeated Across the World
items.snippet: After a fire killed 30 people, the scrutiny has turned to whether the venue used flammable foam on its ceilings and had blocked exits.

items.title: A Heated Rivalry Over Bull Mascots Is Dividing a Brazilian Town
items.snippet: Deep in the Amazon rainforest, a heated rivalry has split a town for a century. It’s got nothing to do with sports or politics.

items.title: Ukraine Wants to Make Its Own Antimissile System, Not Just Patriots
items.snippet: The Ukrainian firm Fire Point has produced weapons that hit Russian territory. Its next goal, developing an antimissile system, is a much bigger challenge, experts said.

items.title: Lebanese Hold Fast to Their Land Despite Threat of Long Israeli Occupation
items.snippet: Entire towns in southern Lebanon have been hollowed out by the war between Israel and Hezbollah. Some residents have stayed, fearing permanent displacement.

items.title: U.K. to Change Law to Allow Deportation of ‘Grooming Gang’ Leader
items.snippet: The British government on Monday laid out steps enabling the deportation to Pakistan of Shabir Ahmed, the leader of a gang that raped dozens of girls in Northern England.

items.title: Japan Builds Intelligence Agency It Hasn’t Had Since World War II
items.snippet: Facing threats from Russia and China, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is turning to help from Western allies in forming a centralized agency.

items.title: The Next Phase in Ukraine’s War With Russia: The Battle for Minds
items.snippet: Ukrainians, already drone innovators, are turning their focus to psychological operations aimed at undercutting Russian support for the war.

items.title: A Robot Army Remakes Ground Warfare in Ukraine
items.snippet: They began as supply mules. Now ground robots evacuate the wounded, hold trenches and even do the killing.

items.title: Residents of Spanish Towns Caught in Fierce Blazes Recount Harrowing Escapes
items.snippet: Many say communication was chaotic and even nonexistent as flames tore through the area, taking 12 lives in one of the deadliest wildfires on record in Spain.

items.title: The Netherlands Pushes to Make Manure a Hotter Product
items.snippet: The Netherlands has been trying to figure out what to do with manure it can no longer use thanks to environmental rules. Volatile fertilizer costs may help.

items.title: Bloomberg Loses Landmark Libel Case in Singapore
items.snippet: A judge ruled in favor of two Singaporean ministers in a closely watched defamation trial seen as a significant test of the country’s libel laws and their implications for press freedom.

lead.title: Pinwheel launches a retro-inspired landline phone for kids
lead.snippet: Kid-friendly tech company Pinwheel announced the launch of a new landline phone designed to let children stay connected without the distractions of a smartphone.

items.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
items.snippet: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.

items.title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be ‘everything for everyone’
items.snippet: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation, and how AI is starting to show up in ways riders and drivers will actually notice.

items.title: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B
items.snippet: With the cash, the company aims to expand its world model offering and reach customers across geographies.

items.title: X just tweaked its algorithm to make it more friendly, less battleground
items.snippet: The social media site says it will amplify posts made by users' mutual followers' to give the feed more of a communal feel.

items.title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
items.snippet: The company is raising at least $75 million, led by Robot Ventures, with significant participation from USV and other prominent investors.

items.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
items.snippet: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary models are somehow acting like Trojan horses.

items.title: Apple says former employee exploited ‘rare’ bug to download confidential files after leaving for OpenAI
items.snippet: Apple would not comment on the "security breach," which allegedly allowed a former employee to download sensitive files from Apple's network long after he departed the company for rival OpenAI.

items.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
items.snippet: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.

items.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
items.snippet: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

items.title: Investors send General Fusion soaring in debut as first publicly traded fusion company
items.snippet: General Fusion started trading on the Nasdaq following a reverse merger that saw high redemptions.

items.title: As TV-tracking app TV Time shuts down, its founder builds Bingers, a new home for fans
items.snippet: The creator of TV Time is building a successor app that will let users import their watch histories and preserve the community that formed around discussing their favorite shows.

lead.title: As White House Leans on Smithsonian, Little Uproar Over Board Vacancies
lead.snippet: An odd silence has surrounded a delay to fill several open seats on the board of the Smithsonian, even as a fierce battle to control the institution ensues.

items.title: Researchers Claim to Have Solved the Perplexing ‘Reverse Sprinkler’ Problem That Stumped Feynman
items.snippet: Physicists have debated which way a submerged sprinkler sucking in water would spin. Careful experiments provide an answer.

items.title: What Happens if Pernambuco Wood Becomes Illegal in Music?
items.snippet: For centuries, the standard wood used to make string instruments’ bows has come from the pernambuco tree in Brazil. Its future is uncertain.

items.title: A Sweet Surprise: Scientists Find Sugar Deep in Our Galaxy
items.snippet: It’s the first time a sugar molecule has been detected in interstellar space. The discovery provides tantalizing new clues into how life may have arisen on Earth.

items.title: A Beastie Boy Returns to the Mic, With Some Help
items.snippet: Thanks to an assist from his sons, Mike D is set to release “Thank You,” the first new music from a member of the pioneering rap trio in 15 years.

items.title: Dark Sky Defenders Raise Alarm Along the Border of West Texas
items.snippet: Residents fear that the Trump administration’s plans to build a border wall will threaten the region’s spectacular starry skies.

items.title: ‘Difficult People’ a Decade Later: A Sarcastic Time Capsule
items.snippet: This cult comedy about two snide show-business outsiders has a new Blu-ray edition, and a reunion this fall. The creator Julie Klausner discusses why she’d love to revive it.

items.title: ‘We’re Fighting Satan’: The War to Save Bees From a Hornet Invasion
items.snippet: As yellow-legged hornets spread through South Carolina’s Lowcountry region, threatening the local honey crop, a state team of bee defenders is racing to the rescue.

items.title: In ‘The Westies,’ Chris Brancato Expands His ‘Scumbag Universe’ (Again)
items.snippet: For his new series, the co-creator of “Narcos” and “Godfather of Harlem” turns his focus to a violent gang of Irish New Yorkers in the early 1980s.

items.title: What Is an Aortic Dissection?
items.snippet: The condition that killed Senator Lindsey Graham is deadly, sudden and difficult to treat — “like a knife to the heart,” one expert said.

items.title: Jay-Z Yankee Stadium Review: Three Nights of Legacy and Guest Stars
items.snippet: The rap mogul convened his starriest collaborators, showed off his still-dazzling skills and made a convincing argument for his singular legacy.

items.title: F.C.C. Approves Test of Space Mirror to Light Night Sky Despite Outcry
items.snippet: A start-up company has permission to try its plan to bounce solar rays onto the dark side of Earth, turning night to day for a three-mile-wide patch.

items.title: A Murder Mystery Evening at Audrey Gelman’s Guesthouse
items.snippet: At her Hudson Valley guesthouse, Audrey Gelman, a co-founder of the Wing, channels her childlike love of play with murder mystery evenings.

items.title: What China’s Successful Rocket Launch Means for the Future of the Space Race
items.snippet: A space neophyte not long ago, China is now the United States’s main competitor for supremacy throughout the solar system.

items.title: Sam Neill’s Best Movie Performances: Where to Stream
items.snippet: From his global breakthrough in “Jurassic Park” to the more recent “Peaky Blinders,” the actor’s range was always on display.
